availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
availableCommands.name: update-config

availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
availableCommands.name: debug

availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
availableCommands.name: simplify

availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
availableCommands.name: batch

availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
availableCommands.name: loop

availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
availableCommands.name: claude-api

availableCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)
availableCommands.name: clear

availableCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
availableCommands.name: compact

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
availableCommands.name: init

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
availableCommands.name: security-review

availableCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
availableCommands.name: usage

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
availableCommands.name: insights

availableCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
availableCommands.name: goal

availableCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
availableCommands.name: team-onboarding

part.delta: then participate as a peer to claim/observe/finalize on

params.prompt:
You are the "planner" peer in a Firegrid choreography experiment arm. There is no central manager.

Run id: 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z
Scenario id: webhook-burst
Arm: choreography
Your peer name: planner

Coordinate ONLY through these Firegrid board channels (use the send / wait_for / wait_for_any tools):
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Watch the board, claim useful work, publish findings, react to peer findings. Do not message me; coordinate with the other peers (builder, reviewer) via the channels above.

Required protocol:
- For every send to a coordination.* channel, payload MUST be a JSON object (not a JSON string).
- Before publishing coordination.final, publish at least one coordination.claims row AND one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row.
- If another peer already produced a final artifact, still publish your finding/review rows.

Tool boundary (HARD):
- Do NOT use shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repository-inspection tools.
- Use only Firegrid session tools and Firegrid channel tools (send, wait_for, wait_for_any).

Task — Webhook Burst Triage:
- Watch coordination.work for inbound webhook incidents.
- Wait for these inbound workIds on coordination.work before finalizing: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-102:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, github:check:failed.
- Use scalar matches such as match.workId for those waits; do not guess payload timestamps.
- Group duplicate incidents by external entity (e.g., the two TF-101 rows are the same Linear issue).
- Triage severity and publish findings on coordination.findings.
- As planner, your angle: outline the dedup grouping + triage plan as a coordination.findings row, then review the builder/reviewer outputs.

Final artifact contract (if you publish it):
- channel: coordination.final
- payload: JSON object with kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", and relevant workId fields
- body under 1,200 characters
- list the deduped incident groups and which evidence rowIds supported each group
- state which evidence you inspected, the recommendation, and any open questions
- do NOT claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why
- after the send returns success, stop

Begin: subscribe to coordination.work with wait_for_any for the four workIds above, publish a claim for your slice, post your planner finding, then observe peer activity.

Final artifact contract (if you publish it):

- payload: JSON object with kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", and relevant workId fields

- state which evidence you inspected, the recommendation, and any open questions
- do NOT claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why
- after the send returns success, stop

Begin: subscribe to coordination.work with wait_for_any for the four workIds above, publish a claim for your slice, then post the dedup grouping finding once you have the evidence.

- list the deduped incident groups and which evidence rowIds supported each group

- do NOT claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why
- after the send returns success, stop

Begin: subscribe to coordination.work with wait_for_any for the four workIds above, claim the "review" slice, then post coordination.reviews rows against the planner/builder findings as they appear.

part.delta: All four evidence rows in. Publishing claim, finding,

params.payload: {"kind": "claim", "claimantSessionId": "conductor", "title": "Conductor peer: dedup grouping + final synthesis", "workId": "webhook-burst:triage:dedup", "status": "claimed", "body": "Conductor peer claiming the cross-evidence dedup grouping slice across linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, linear:TF-102:a, github:check:failed. Will publish a finding and a final artifact; peers (planner/builder/reviewer) are welcome to publish their own claims, findings, reviews, and finals in parallel — the runner measures board behavior, not just the first final."}

rawInput.payload: {"kind": "claim", "claimantSessionId": "conductor", "title": "Conductor peer: dedup grouping + final synthesis", "workId": "webhook-burst:triage:dedup", "status": "claimed", "body": "Conductor peer claiming the cross-evidence dedup grouping slice across linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, linear:TF-102:a, github:check:failed. Will publish a finding and a final artifact; peers (planner/builder/reviewer) are welcome to publish their own claims, findings, reviews, and finals in parallel — the runner measures board behavior, not just the first final."}

params.payload:
{"kind": "finding", "title": "Webhook burst dedup grouping (3 groups, 4 evidence rows)", "workId": "webhook-burst:triage:dedup", "status": "open", "body": "Evidence inspected (coordination.work rowIds):
- 520c320d-... workId=linear:TF-101:a — TF-101 label bug→regression, atMs=0
- 60be49da-... workId=linear:TF-101:duplicate — duplicate delivery same external id, atMs=500
- 80d09943-... workId=linear:TF-102:a — TF-102 customer escalation comment, atMs=250
- c8458570-... workId=github:check:failed — CI failed, signature mentions 'unknown-channel', atMs=1500

Deduped groups:
G1 Linear TF-101 regression: [520c320d (TF-101:a), 60be49da (TF-101:duplicate)] — suppress duplicate, keep TF-101:a as canonical. Severity: medium (regression label).
G2 Linear TF-102 escalation: [80d09943 (TF-102:a)] — severity: high (customer escalation).
G3 GitHub CI failure: [c8458570 (github:check:failed)] — severity: medium; 'unknown-channel' signature may correlate with G1 regression — flag for confirmation, do not auto-merge.

Recommendation: dedup by external entity id (linear issue key) + delivery id; cross-source links require an explicit signature match, not source proximity."}

{"kind": "final", "title": "Webhook burst triage — deduped groups + recommendation", "status": "complete", "workId": "webhook-burst:triage:dedup", "body": "Evidence inspected (coordination.work rowIds):
